article_title: Qualcomm Launches First Wi-Fi 8 Chip at MWC 2026 as 40-Company Coalition Commits to 6G by 2029
summary: Three factual issues require correction: Apple is incorrectly listed as a 6G coalition member, Dragonwing NPro A8 Elite specs are misattributed to BGR, and specific 6G OTA technical parameters (30 kHz subcarrier spacing, 6-8 GHz range, 4 antennas) are not confirmed by the official press release.

findings.details:
androidauthority.com: https://www.androidauthority.com/qualcomm-fastconnect-8800-reveal-3645483/
notebookcheck.net: https://www.notebookcheck.net/Fastest-and-longest-range-device-Wi-Fi-ever-Qualcomm-debuts-new-FastConnect-8800-Wi-Fi-8-chip-and-X105-5G-Modem-RF.1239326.0.html
bgr.com: https://www.bgr.com/2113109/wi-fi-8-chip-announced-release-date-details/
telecomtv.com: https://www.telecomtv.com/content/6g/qualcomm-and-ericsson-move-6g-from-concept-to-proof-setting-a-clear-path-to-commercialization-54951/
telecomtv.com: https://www.telecomtv.com/content/6g/mwc26-ericsson-claims-6g-world-first-54956/
thefastmode.com: https://www.thefastmode.com/technology-solutions/47295-ericsson-apple-mediatek-to-build-6g-ecosystem-with-live-5g-6g-spectrum-sharing-demos-at-mwc-2026

findings.message: Apple incorrectly listed as 6G coalition member
findings.details: The article states the coalition signatories include Apple (body text: 'The signatories include Airtel, Amazon, Apple, Asus...'). The summary also says 'a 40-company coalition including Ericsson, Apple, and Google'. However, the official Qualcomm press release (BusinessWire, March 1 2026) and independent reporting (Telecompaper) confirm the full coalition member list, which does NOT include Apple. Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Samsung, Nokia, and Ericsson are all confirmed members, but Apple is absent from the announcement. The Ericsson/Apple/MediaTek collaboration for 5G/6G spectrum sharing demos is a separate partnership from the 40-company 6G commercialization coalition.

findings.message: Dragonwing NPro A8 Elite specs misattributed to BGR
findings.details: The article attributes the Dragonwing NPro A8 Elite specs (5x5 Wi-Fi 8 radio, 40% higher throughput, 2.5x lower latency, 30% lower energy consumption) to BGR (https://www.bgr.com/2113109/wi-fi-8-chip-announced-release-date-details/). However, the BGR article does not mention the Dragonwing NPro A8 Elite or any router-side platform specs — it focuses solely on the FastConnect 8800 mobile chip. The Dragonwing NPro A8 Elite specs are real (confirmed via web search and Qualcomm's announcement) but must be attributed to a source that actually reports these figures.

findings.message: Specific 6G OTA technical parameters not confirmed by official sources
findings.details: The article states the OTA demonstration used '400 MHz component carrier with 30 kHz subcarrier spacing in the centimeter-wave range (approximately 6–8 GHz), with four transmit-receive antennas on the device side.' The official Ericsson/Qualcomm press release (PRNewswire) confirms '400 MHz' and '7GHz range (Centimeter Wave)' but does not specify 30 kHz subcarrier spacing or 4 transmit-receive antennas. These granular parameters should either be removed or traced to a specific primary source that confirms them.

content_preview.title: Qualcomm Launches First Wi-Fi 8 Chip at MWC 2026 as 40-Company Coalition Commits to 6G by 2029
content_preview.summary: Qualcomm's FastConnect 8800 doubles Wi-Fi speeds to 11.6 Gbps while a 40-company coalition including Ericsson, Apple, and Google formally targets 6G commercialization starting 2029.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Mobile World Congress 2026, running March 2–5 in Barcelona, opened with two significant wireless connectivity announcements. Qualcomm introduced the FastConnect 8800, its first chip supporting Wi-Fi 8, claiming double the speeds and triple the range of its previous generation. Separately, a coalition of over 40 industry leaders — including Ericsson, Apple, Google, Microsoft, Samsung, and Nokia — formally committed to launching commercial 6G networks beginning in 2029, backed by live...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-researched and well-structured article covering two major MWC 2026 wireless announcements. Technical depth is appropriate for a News category piece. The article correctly covers the FastConnect 8800 specs, Wi-Fi 8 architectural improvements, and the 6G commercialization coalition. The 'What We Don't Know' and 'Analysis' sections add genuine editorial value. Word count (1053) falls within the News category range (400-1200).
editor_notes.source_verification: 7 sources cited, all HTTPS. Android Authority, NotebookCheck, BGR, TelecomTV (x2), The Fast Mode, and Engadget are all reputable tech publications. The Engadget source covers MWC 2026 broadly. However, the BGR article does not contain the Dragonwing NPro A8 Elite specs attributed to it, and the TelecomTV coalition article does not confirm the 40-member list with Apple included. The sources are legitimate outlets but one attribution is incorrect.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Three issues identified through manual source verification: (1) Apple is incorrectly listed as a 6G coalition signatory — the official Qualcomm press release and Telecompaper both confirm the coalition member list which excludes Apple. Apple's involvement in MWC 2026 6G activity is limited to the separate Ericsson/Apple/MediaTek spectrum sharing demo, which is a different announcement. (2) The Dragonwing NPro A8 Elite specs are attributed to BGR but BGR does not cover this product — these stats need a corrected source citation. (3) The 6G OTA demo technical parameters of '30 kHz subcarrier spacing' and 'four transmit-receive antennas' are not confirmed by the official press release, which only states '400 MHz' and '7GHz range (Centimeter Wave)'.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism or editorializing. Technical claims are appropriately qualified with phrases like 'the company claims' and 'according to'. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: Confirmed original. No existing Machine Herald articles cover Qualcomm FastConnect 8800, Wi-Fi 8, or the 6G coalition. This is new coverage of MWC 2026 announcements from March 2, 2026.
concerns: Apple is incorrectly listed as a member of Qualcomm's 40-company 6G commercialization coalition. Per the official Qualcomm press release (BusinessWire, March 1 2026) and corroborating sources, Apple is not in the coalition member list. This is a factual error that misrepresents who committed to the 2029 6G roadmap. The summary line also incorrectly states 'a 40-company coalition including Ericsson, Apple, and Google'.

The Dragonwing NPro A8 Elite stats (40% throughput gain, 2.5x latency reduction, 30% energy savings) are attributed to BGR but BGR's article does not mention this product. The stats are likely real but need to be attributed to a source that actually reports them (e.g., AndroidCentral, TechRadar, or Qualcomm's own press release).

The specific 6G OTA technical parameters '30 kHz subcarrier spacing' and 'four transmit-receive antennas on the device side' are not confirmed by the official press release. Either source these parameters to a primary document or remove the unverified specifics.
recommendations: Remove Apple from the 6G coalition member list in the body text and from the article summary. Replace with an accurate framing: the coalition does not include Apple, though Apple is separately involved in Ericsson's 5G/6G spectrum sharing demos at MWC 2026.

Replace the BGR citation for the Dragonwing NPro A8 Elite paragraph with a source that actually reports those specs. Candidates: Android Central (https://www.androidcentral.com/phones/qualcomm/qualcomms-wi-fi-8-chips-are-here-and-they-actually-fix-the-stuff-that-annoys-you), TechRadar (https://www.techradar.com/computing/wi-fi-broadband/faster-speeds-higher-reliability-longer-range-and-powerful-ai-qualcomm-introduces-wi-fi-8-chips-with-a-startling-speed-boost-at-mwc-2026), or Qualcomm's official press release.

Either remove the '30 kHz subcarrier spacing' and 'four transmit-receive antennas' claims from the 6G OTA section, or source them to a document that explicitly states these values. The confirmed facts are: 400 MHz carrier bandwidth, centimeter-wave at ~7 GHz, AI and cloud-native architecture.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong article with good structure, appropriate depth, and credible sources. Three factual corrections are required before publication: removing Apple from the 6G coalition list, fixing the misattributed Dragonwing source citation, and removing or sourcing the unverified 6G OTA technical parameters. These are substantive errors that touch on the accuracy of coalition membership claims and source attribution integrity.
